name: Hot and Sour Soup (Chinese)
recipeIngredient: 1 Heaping tablespoon small tree ear mushrooms
1/4 lb Lean pork, cut into matchstick shreds
1/4 cup Bamboo shoots, cut into matchstick shreds
3 tbsp Cornstarch mixed with 1/4 cup water
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the mushrooms, tree ear mushrooms, and dried lily buds in a bowl and cover with boiling water. Let stand for 30 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, slice the firm bean curd into strips and set aside.
3. Combine the pork shreds with the dark soy sauce in a small bowl.
4. Combine the light soy sauce, white vinegar, and salt in another bowl.
5. Remove the black mushrooms from the soaking liquid, cut off the woody stems, and discard. Slice the caps thinly and put into a bowl.
6. Drain and rinse the tree ear mushrooms, then add them to the bowl with the other mushrooms.
7. Remove the dried lily buds, cut off the woody tips, slice them in two, pull each apart by hand into 2 or 3 shreds, and add them to the bowl with the mushrooms.
8. Heat the peanut oil in a wok or large pot. Stir-fry the pork until it changes color.
9. Add the tree ear mixture and bamboo shoots; cook, stirring for 1 minute.
10. Add the chicken stock and bring to a boil (you may have this simmering in a separate pot). Stir in the vinegar mixture.
11. Adjust the seasonings as needed.
12. Cook until boiling, then add the bean curd.
13. When boiling, stir the cornstarch/water mixture to recombine and stir into the soup.
14. Cook until the soup thickens and clears slightly.
15. Turn off the heat and slowly swirl in the beaten eggs.
16. Transfer to a soup tureen, sprinkle with white pepper, drizzle with sesame oil, garnish with scallions and coriander, and serve.
